source_name: UK Statutory RSHE — Full Programme (DfE July 2025)
domain_type_notes: RSHE is unusual: predominantly propositional-hierarchical (T1: 70% of KUD — laws, facts, health science) with embedded relational skills (T2: 24%) and a small dispositional component (T3: 6%). Domain declared as dispositional per --dispositional flag (relationships domain); T1 dominance is a source-fidelity finding, not a classification error. Health sections (puberty, nutrition, first aid) are explicitly hierarchical. Relationship sections (consent, ethics, online safety) are horizontal/dispositional.
hierarchical_elements: Health Protection and Prevention / medical facts (T1)
Legal Knowledge / marriage law, consent law, FGM law (T1)
contestation_notes: July 2025 statutory guidance replaces the withdrawn 2019 version. Sex education content remains politically contested in England; parental withdrawal rights are statutory. The 2025 guidance is more politically settled than the 2019 version but retains ideological framing (e.g. emphasis on marriage and civil partnership, age-appropriate teaching provisions). Schools have limited flexibility on compulsory elements.
developmental_scope_notes: Document uses two explicit phases: 'content to be covered by the end of primary' and 'content to be covered by the end of secondary'. This is the document's own two-phase architecture, not the four-KS National Curriculum structure. Neither phase is subdivided into KS1/KS2 or KS3/KS4 within curriculum content sections. Band reference: End of Primary (ages 5-11, KS1+KS2) and End of Secondary (ages 11-16, KS3+KS4).

End of Primary.description: Primary Relationships Education + Primary Health and Wellbeing

End of Secondary.description: Secondary RSE + Secondary Health and Wellbeing

gate_results_summary.type3_distribution: FAIL (informational) — Type 3 rate is 6.1% vs expected ≥20% for dispositional domain. RSHE is principally a statutory knowledge curriculum (knowing rights, laws, health facts) with embedded relational skills (T2) and minimal dispositional outcomes (T3). The low T3 rate reflects the source's propositional framing, not a classification failure. This gate triggers informational only — not a halt.

statements.statement: I can express my basic needs and feelings clearly while recognizing simple personal boundaries.
I can communicate assertively across various situations while establishing and maintaining appropriate boundaries through effective emotional regulation.

flags_for_human_review.detail: 8 rubrics with gate failures (5 generation-unreliable placeholders, 3 semantic gate failures: observable_verb, single_construct x2). Supporting components not generated for these 8 LTs.
